meaning: n.戴奥尼夏,为叙拉古(Syracuse)的暴君
examples: Milton's quoting Dionysius Alexandrinus in this passage.
Dionysius actually speaks of the council in the heroic age as composed of nobles.
The odor of wild thyme, Pliny tells us, drives away snakes. Dionysius of Syracuse, on the other hand, thinks it an aphrodisiac.
Abbot Dionysius made his calculations before the concept of year 0 was invented.
According to the Pope, Christ was born years earlier than Dionysius Exiguus - Dennis the Small in English - had thought.
